page: Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Dnlv6 family. It is equipped with 32 dedicated virtual CPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. This configuration comprises 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, Intel AMX for tile and integer operations, and hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA extensions.

The server is provisioned with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per virtual core. This low-memory configuration is optimized for network-intensive workloads rather than memory-heavy applications. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, requiring external block storage attachment. It operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in specific areas, notably in large-block read-only memory bandwidth at 202.93 GB/sec and static web serving throughput at 46.21 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25% tier. Other metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance, fall within the average range. The server demonstrates a qualitative cost efficiency profile suited for compute-heavy tasks that do not require massive memory footprints, balancing high CPU clock speeds against a lower RAM-to-vCPU ratio.

This hardware profile is suited for network-optimized tasks, static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and compute-bound workloads that fit within a 64 GB memory boundary. It is also applicable for cryptography tasks and workloads utilizing Intel AMX instructions.
description: Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is an x86_64 server in the Dnlv6 family, featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, it includes 16 physical cores, a 260 MB L3 cache, and support for AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. This network-optimized, low-memory instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on external premium storage. In benchmarks, it exhibits strong performance in static web serving throughput at 46.21 GB/sec and large-block read-only memory bandwidth at 202.93 GB/sec, while CPU and database operations fall in the average range. This resource density makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound and network-heavy applications that do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios. It is suited for static web serving, cryptography, and multi-threaded decompression workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is a network-optimized server with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong static web serving and large-block memory read performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is a network-optimized server featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and strong static web serving performance.
tagline: A network-optimized, low-memory compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors with advanced matrix extensions for efficient parallel processing.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio
Strong static web serving throughput of 46.21 GB/sec
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Support for AVX-512 and Intel AMX instruction sets
Suited for network-optimized tasks, static web serving, and cryptography
